content.full_text:
Re:
Fern Polrrrcnr- PRACrrcEs C oMMrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
Febnrary 9,2010
Bruce W. Ebert
300 Harding Blvd, Ste 118
Roseville, CA 95648
Your Request for Advice
Our file No. A-09-053a
Dear Mr. Ebert:
We have received and reviewed your request for reconsideration of Advice Letter,
No. 4-09-053 provided to you on March 17,2009, and the additional facts you provided. Our
prior letter was based on the facts you presented, mainly that as the President of the Board of
Psychology, you reviewed an Administrative Law Judge's ("ALI") decision regarding a license
revocation. To make its determination whether to accept or reject the ALI's determination, the
Board reviewed the information that the ALI provided regarding the case. Based on the facts
and conclusions the ALI presented, the Board made the ultimate determination on whether or not
to revoke the license. In your request for reconsideration, you provided information that to
reinstate the license, the Board must consider, inter alia, "the nature and severity of the act(s) or
offense(s)" and "the total criminal record." To do this, the Board must consider the facts
involved in the revocation proceeding. The Board therefore will be considering the same facts
about the same party that was previously before it and we thus consider this to be the same
proceeding for purposes of the Political Reform Act's "permanent ban" under Govemment Code
Sections 87400-87404.
Our advice on questions concerning Government Code Sections 87400-87404 is made on
a case by case basis and considers the particplar facts of each case. The "permanent ban" applies
to prevent a former state official from leaving state service and representing a party for
compensation on the same proceeding he or she worked while in state service. You made a
decision while in state service that you now seek to be paid to reverse. For the reasons stated in
our prior letter, the "permanent ban" applies and we reaffirm our advice in that letter.


Our File No. A-09-053a
pageZ
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
llr'il, llflilo^-.
By: Heather M. Rowan
Commission Counsel
Legal Division
HMRjgl

sections.conclusion: Our advice on questions concerning Government Code Sections 87400-87404 is made on a case by case basis and considers the particular facts of each case. The 'permanent ban' applies to prevent a former state official from leaving state service and representing a party for compensation on the same proceeding he or she worked while in state service. You made a decision while in state service that you now seek to be paid to reverse. For the reasons stated in our prior letter, the 'permanent ban' applies and we reaffirm our advice in that letter.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the 'permanent ban' under Government Code Sections 87400-87404 applies to a former state official (President of the Board of Psychology) who seeks to be compensated for reversing a license revocation decision they made while in state service.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC affirms its prior advice that the permanent ban under Government Code Sections 87400-87404 applies to the requestor. The requestor cannot be compensated for attempting to reverse a license revocation decision made while serving as President of the Board of Psychology, as this constitutes working on the same proceeding for compensation after leaving state service.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a reconsideration letter responding to a prior advice letter dated March 17, 2009. The document lacks explicit 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers but contains clear question and conclusion content. Minor OCR issues present (e.g., 'Febnrary' for February, 'particplar' for particular) but do not affect meaning.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the 'permanent ban' under Government Code Sections 87400-87404 applies to a former state official (President of the Board of Psychology) who seeks to be compensated for reversing a license revocation decision they made while in state service.

CONCLUSION: Our advice on questions concerning Government Code Sections 87400-87404 is made on a case by case basis and considers the particular facts of each case. The 'permanent ban' applies to prevent a former state official from leaving state service and representing a party for compensation on the same proceeding he or she worked while in state service. You made a decision while in state service that you now seek to be paid to reverse. For the reasons stated in our prior letter, the 'permanent ban' applies and we reaffirm our advice in that letter.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC response to a request for reconsideration of Advice Letter No. 4-09-053 regarding whether the permanent ban on former state officials applies to a former Board of Psychology President seeking compensation to reverse a license revocation decision. The FPPC reaffirms its prior advice that the permanent ban applies.
